typecheck.command: xcodebuild build-for-testing -project OpenBurnBar.xcodeproj -scheme OpenBurnBar -destination "platform=macOS,arch=arm64" -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .spm-cache -derivedDataPath .derived-data/ci-typecheck CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify mission-path guidance in worker skills so mission-scoped `validation-contract.md` location is explicit and workers do not incorrectly search only repo-root paths.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review for `misc-daemon-list-tiebreak-fixture-hardening` found transcript evidence of repo-root-only contract path checks despite the mission-scoped contract path existing and being authoritative.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit CI fallback guidance for app test execution in `operator-ui-worker` to align with mission AGENTS constraints where non-CI app tests may hang.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review for `misc-m2-authoring-quality-hardening` observed timed app-test command attempts and ad-hoc fallback behavior not captured in the skill guidance.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Normalize governance skill validation commands to derive from `.factory/services.yaml` only, and remove duplicated inline command catalogs to prevent drift.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review for `misc-governance-worker-evidence-rigor-guidance` identified command-name drift (`OpenBurnBarMissionControlContractsTests` vs `BurnBarMissionControlContractsTests`) and duplicated command lists in skill content.

rejectedObservations.observation: Mission guidance says workers should use `.factory/services.yaml`, but skill procedures still embed inline command variants that can drift.
